methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Maritime Fusion is a Seed-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector founded by Justin Cohen and Jason Kaufmann. The company has raised $4.5M to date. The Climate & Energy universe contains nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), though a narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints for median-based valuation.

Given data constraints, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised of $4M as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement, the method implies an $11M valuation. This represents the single applicable triangulation approach given the absence of three or more same-stage peers within the Climate & Energy sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $13M, centered on $11M. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier to Maritime Fusion's specific risk profile and growth trajectory, the limited comparability of broader Climate & Energy peers (Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen), and the absence of transaction data or revenue metrics. Further narrowing would require additional same-stage company datapoints or direct assessment of product development milestones and go-to-market readiness.
